libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set JGroups-related system properties before constructing channels or touching LogFactory: jgroups.bind_addr=127.0.0.1, java.net.preferIPv4Stack=true, and preferably jgroups.use.jdk_logger=true. Use in-process/loopback stacks such as SHARED_LOOPBACK or loopback TCP/UDP configurations; avoid relying on host multicast, DNS_PING, JDBC_PING against a real database, Kubernetes/cloud discovery, or external cluster nodes unless a test explicitly provides those services.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8866 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.jgroups:jgroups:5.2.2.Final; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Optional logging backends such as SLF4J or Log4j2 are probed only when present on the classpath; adding them changes the reachability surface and is not required for core JGroups coverage.
WildFly OpenSSL is optional for SSL_KEY_EXCHANGE acceleration; JDK SSL is sufficient for meaningful coverage unless the workflow intentionally targets native OpenSSL support.
Tests that use default UDP multicast or environment-dependent bind addresses may pass locally but be flaky or fail under CI/native-image.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: JGroups has no required runtime dependencies or external service requirement, but meaningful and stable tests should force loopback IPv4/JDK logging before JGroups initializes.
